reasoning: Spec for `new` only specifies when result is Err (three invalid-input conditions) and what holds if Ok, but never guarantees Ok for valid inputs. An implementation returning Err unconditionally satisfies all four ensures clauses. This is the canonical liveness gap.
Duplicate of phi_neg_1. Same spec, same gap (missing liveness), just instantiated at number_of_bits==8. No additional finding.
Duplicate of phi_neg_1. Same spec, same gap, instantiated at number_of_bits==256.
Duplicate of phi_neg_1. Same spec, same gap, instantiated at u32::MAX-7. No new finding beyond the missing liveness guarantee already captured by phi_neg_1.

Tautological — assumptions are contradictory. The assume requires array@.len()*8 == u32::MAX-1 (=4294967294), but 4294967294 is not divisible by 8, so no integer length satisfies this. Verus proves the ensures vacuously via ex falso. The from_raw_array spec actually DOES guarantee Ok (unconditional Ok in ensures), so there is no real gap.
Duplicate of phi_neg_1. Identical spec, identical gap (missing liveness for `new`), identical assumption structure (valid inputs + result is Err). Different description but same finding.
Duplicate of phi_neg_1. The OOM narrative adds motivating context but demonstrates the identical spec gap — no liveness clause in `new`. The assumption structure is identical to phi_neg_1/phi_gap_4.
Duplicate of phi_neg_1 and phi_neg_1_minimal. Same spec, same gap, same concrete value (number_of_bits==8).

Tautological — the body assumes size==0, which directly contradicts the requires clause (size > 0). The requires is available inside the proof body, giving Verus both size>0 and size==0 simultaneously — a contradiction. Verified vacuously via ex falso; no real spec gap.
